topic.title: Making the Most Out of Guest Lectures
topic.text:
Hi everyone,

I wanted to share a quick update on grading for the guest lecture extra credit, along with some genuine advice I hope you'll take to heart.

You may have noticed that grading for these sessions has become stricter. We've been doing random participation checks throughout each lecture, and if you're found off-camera on 2 or more occasions, your credit for that session will be reduced.

We've also noticed that some questions are being typed into the chat even when there's time to ask the guest directly. The guests are there, live, ready to engage with you, please take advantage of that. A typed question in the chat doesn't give you the same experience as a real conversation.

I'd also gently encourage you to think about what these sessions actually are. Professors Nik and Nina put in a lot of time and effort to bring in industry professionals who would otherwise be really hard to get access to. These guests give up their weekends to meet with you and invest in your success. That's not a small thing.

As a fellow student, if I may give you a piece of advice, many of these professionals are active in hiring communities. Showing up, being engaged, and asking thoughtful questions leaves an impression, and that impression can follow you into your job search. This is one of those rare opportunities that's worth a lot more than the points attached to it.

Hope to see everyone engaged and present at the next session.

 

Best,
Ari Bandopadhyaya
Teaching Assistant, INFO 7375
